Vendor setup · Intelligence · On-device

Set up Apple Intelligence

Apple Intelligence shapes your transcript into your note using Apple's built-in on-device Foundation Models — no account, no API key, no per-token cost. In its default mode the note is shaped entirely on your Mac and the transcript never leaves the device.

Intelligence (note-shaping) On-device — no BAA needed No API cost

Role in DirectScribe
Intelligence — molds the transcript into your note template.
Where the text goes
In On-device mode, nowhere: Apple's model runs on your Mac, so the transcript never leaves the device.
Privacy paperwork
None for on-device use — there is no external vendor to sign an agreement with.
Cost
No API fee. It uses your Mac's built-in model.
Requirements
A Mac on macOS 26 or later with Apple Intelligence turned on in System Settings, on Apple-Intelligence-capable hardware.

Apple Intelligence covers note-shaping, not transcription. At launch, transcription is still a cloud step — pair Apple Intelligence with a transcription vendor such as Deepgram.

Turn it on

  1. Enable Apple Intelligence

    In macOS System Settings → Apple Intelligence & Siri, turn Apple Intelligence on. The on-device model downloads once; DirectScribe shows its readiness in Settings.

  2. Choose it in DirectScribe

    Open Settings → Providers → Intelligence and choose Apple Intelligence. There is no key to enter.

  3. Pick a processing mode

    Choose how notes are processed:

    • On-device only (default) — the note is shaped entirely on your Mac; the transcript never leaves the device.
    • Automatic — on-device first, falling back to Apple's Private Cloud Compute only if the on-device model can't handle a note.
    • Private Cloud Compute — always use Apple's private servers.

Private Cloud Compute — planned

The Automatic and Private Cloud Compute modes rely on Apple's Private Cloud Compute (Apple's privacy-preserving server inference), which requires macOS 27 and is not enabled in the current build. Until then, DirectScribe runs Apple Intelligence fully on-device. When Private Cloud Compute is used, DirectScribe shows a clear "Processed with Private Cloud Compute" indicator and records it in the transparency log — so you always know whether a note left your Mac.

Contact us How on-device paths are handled
DirectScribe

A Mac-native, bring-your-own-API-key dictation app for physicians in private practice across Canada. Built by a physician.

Not legal advice. DirectScribe is a documentation tool. Compliance materials on this site are general information to help you and your privacy advisor do your own assessment under PIPEDA and your province's health-privacy law (e.g., PHIPA in Ontario). They are not legal advice and do not create any professional relationship.

© 2026 DirectScribe · Made for macOS · This site runs no scripts and sets no cookies. · directscribe.ca
